agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on capital grant for child care at Brentwood Park Elementary
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a one-time capital grant from the Community Benefit Bonus Reserve to the Burnaby School District. The grant would fund construction of a child care facility at Brentwood Park Elementary School (1455 Delta Avenue). This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.1 APPROVAL OF CAPITAL GRANT FUNDING FOR CHILD CARE ON SCHOOL SITE
Purpose:  To provide updated information on provincial funding received and request Council approval of a one-time capital grant to the Burnaby School District from the Community Benefit Bonus Reserve for the construction of a child care facility at the Brentwood Park Elementary School site (1455 Delta Avenue).
agenda_briefs.article_title: Financial Management Committee Meeting Agenda — July 21, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on new child care centre operating agreements
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on authorizing new Licence and Operating Agreements for five City-owned child care centres. A 'Licence and Operating Agreement' is a contract that lets a child care provider run a centre on city property. If approved, this would allow the City to sign new deals with the operators of these centres. This is a proposed decision — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if you use or operate a city-owned child care centre.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.2 CITY-OWNED CHILD CARE CENTRES NEW LICENCE AND OPERATING AGREEMENTS
Purpose:  To obtain Council authorization to enter into new Licence and Operating Agreements for five City-owned child care centres.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Financial Management Committee Meeting Agenda — July 21, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider status update on Burnaby civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will review a status update on significant civic projects at an upcoming meeting. This report, dated July 2026, provides an overview of how major city-building projects are progressing. The update is for information only — no immediate decisions or bylaw changes are being proposed. However, business owners should watch for future council decisions that could affect construction timelines, road closures, or development opportunities in the city.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1 SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - JULY 2026
Purpose:  To provide a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Financial Management Committee Meeting Agenda — July 21, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review IT initiatives update for April–June 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the city's IT initiatives for the period of April to June 2026. This is a routine report that outlines progress on ongoing technology projects, such as system upgrades, cybersecurity improvements, and digital services for residents and businesses. The update is for information only — no new rules or fees are proposed at this time. However, business owners who rely on city online services (e.g., permits, licensing, or tax payments) should be aware that future IT changes could affect how they interact with the city.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2 SU: IT INITIATIVES APRIL - JUNE 2026
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) of IT initiatives for April to June 2026.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Financial Management Committee Meeting Agenda — July 21, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review major engineering project updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on major engineering projects from May to July 2026. This is a proposed review of ongoing work, not a final decision. The update covers projects like road, sewer, and water main improvements that could affect local businesses through construction delays, road closures, or utility work. Business owners should watch for specific project details that may impact their operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor the council meeting agenda for specific project locations and timelines that could affect your business access or operations.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3 SU: MAJOR ENGINEERING PROJECTS - MAY 2026 - JULY 2026
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on major Engineering Projects.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Financial Management Committee Meeting Agenda — July 21, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby's financial report for Period 5 of 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will review a financial report comparing the City's actual spending and revenue for the first five months of 2026 against the 2026-2030 Financial Plan that was approved on February 10, 2026. This is a routine check-in to see if the city is on track with its budget. No new rules or fees are proposed in this report itself, but it may lead to future budget adjustments that could affect business owners through changes in property taxes, business licence fees, or city services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4 FINANCIAL REPORT AS AT 2026 PERIOD 05
Purpose: To provide an overview of the City’s financial activity as of the end of Period 05 and to compare that activity with the 2026 - 2030 Financial Plan adopted by Council on February 10, 2026.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Financial Management Committee Meeting Agenda — July 21, 2026
